name: config
context: Initialize and configure turbo.json — global options, env modes, remote cache, and per-workspace overrides
info: This skill covers creating and configuring turbo.json, the central configuration file for Turborepo. It includes global options (globalDependencies, globalEnv, envMode, ui, concurrency), remote cache setup (TURBO_TOKEN, TURBO_TEAM, remoteCache object), per-workspace configuration overrides using the extends field, and environment variable handling in strict vs loose mode. Agents use this skill when setting up a new Turborepo project, migrating configuration, or tuning build behavior. See [[turbo/pipelines]] for task definitions within turbo.json, [[turbo/workspaces]] for monorepo structure, and [[turbo/caching]] for cache-specific configuration.
triggers: set turbo env mode to strict
body:
# Turborepo Configuration

All Turborepo configuration lives in `turbo.json` at the repository root. Workspaces can override task-level settings with their own `turbo.json` using the `extends` field.

## Root turbo.json Structure

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://turborepo.dev/schema.json",
  "globalDependencies": [".env", "tsconfig.base.json"],
  "globalEnv": ["NODE_ENV", "CI"],
  "globalPassThroughEnv": ["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"],
  "envMode": "strict",
  "ui": "stream",
  "concurrency": 10,
  "cacheDir": ".turbo/cache",
  "tasks": {
    "build": {
      "dependsOn": ["^build"],
      "outputs": ["dist/**"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Global Options Reference

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `globalDependencies` | `string[]` | `[]` | Files that invalidate all task caches when changed |
| `globalEnv` | `string[]` | `[]` | Env vars included in every task hash |
| `globalPassThroughEnv` | `string[]` | `null` | Env vars available to tasks but not hashed (strict mode) |
| `envMode` | `"strict"\|"loose"` | `"strict"` | Controls env var availability |
| `ui` | `"tui"\|"stream"` | `"stream"` | Terminal output mode |
| `concurrency` | `number\|string` | `10` | Max parallel tasks (number or percentage like "50%") |
| `cacheDir` | `string` | `".turbo/cache"` | Local cache directory |
| `cacheMaxAge` | `string` | `"0"` | Max age of cache entries (e.g., "7d", "24h") |
| `cacheMaxSize` | `string` | `"0"` | Max total cache size (e.g., "10GB") |
| `dangerouslyDisablePackageManagerCheck` | `boolean` | `false` | Skip package manager validation |
| `noUpdateNotifier` | `boolean` | `false` | Suppress update notifications |

## Environment Mode

**Strict mode** (default, recommended): Only explicitly declared env vars (via `env`, `globalEnv`, `passThroughEnv`, `globalPassThroughEnv`) are available to tasks. This prevents cache pollution from undeclared variables.

**Loose mode**: All environment variables are available. Risks cache invalidation issues.

```json
{
  "envMode": "strict",
  "globalEnv": ["NODE_ENV", "CI"],
  "tasks": {
    "build": {
      "env": ["API_URL", "DATABASE_*"],
      "passThroughEnv": ["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"]
    }
  }
}
```

Env patterns support wildcards (`API_*`) and negation (`!API_SECRET`).

## Remote Cache Configuration

Enable remote caching to share build artifacts across CI and team members:

```bash
# Authenticate with Vercel (default provider)
npx turbo login
npx turbo link
```

Or configure manually via environment variables:
```bash
export TURBO_TOKEN="your-token"
export TURBO_TEAM="your-team"
```

Advanced remote cache options in turbo.json:
```json
{
  "remoteCache": {
    "enabled": true,
    "signature": false,
    "preflight": false,
    "timeout": 30,
    "uploadTimeout": 60
  }
}
```

## Per-Workspace Overrides

Create a `turbo.json` in any workspace to override task configuration:

```json
{
  "extends": ["//"],
  "tasks": {
    "build": {
      "outputs": [".next/**", "!.next/cache/**"]
    }
  }
}
```

The `"//"` syntax means "extend from the root turbo.json". Only task-level fields can be overridden — global options are always inherited from root.

## CI Configuration

For CI environments, set these environment variables:

```yaml
# GitHub Actions example
env:
  TURBO_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }}
  TURBO_TEAM: ${{ vars.TURBO_TEAM }}
```

This enables remote caching in CI without `turbo login`.

sections.title: Root turbo.json
sections.body: The root turbo.json is required and defines global configuration plus task definitions. It must exist at the repository root alongside package.json. Add `$schema` for editor autocompletion. Global fields like `globalDependencies`, `globalEnv`, and `envMode` affect all tasks. Task definitions live under the `tasks` key — see [[turbo/pipelines]] for details.

sections.title: Environment variable modes
sections.body: Strict mode (default) only exposes env vars declared in `env`, `globalEnv`, `passThroughEnv`, or `globalPassThroughEnv` to tasks. This ensures cache correctness — undeclared variables cannot silently invalidate or pollute the cache. Loose mode passes all env vars through but risks cache hits returning stale results when an undeclared variable changes. Always prefer strict mode and declare env vars explicitly.

sections.title: Remote cache setup
sections.body: Remote caching shares task outputs across machines. Vercel provides built-in remote caching — run `turbo login` then `turbo link`. For CI, set `TURBO_TOKEN` and `TURBO_TEAM` environment variables. Self-hosted remote caches are possible via the Remote Caching API. Enable artifact signing with `TURBO_REMOTE_CACHE_SIGNATURE_KEY` for supply chain security. The `remoteCache` object in turbo.json controls timeouts, preflight requests, and signature verification.

sections.title: Per-workspace configuration
sections.body: Any workspace can create its own turbo.json with `"extends": ["//"]` to override task-level configuration from the root. This is useful when a specific app has different outputs (e.g., Next.js `.next/**` vs Vite `dist/**`) or needs additional env vars. Only task-level fields can be overridden; global fields are always inherited from root. The `$TURBO_EXTENDS$` token appends to parent arrays instead of replacing them.
